title: shorea gardneri
intro: Shorea gardneri, also known as the Borneo ironwood or balau, is a species of tree in the family Dipterocarpaceae. It is valued for its rich, dark red wood that is highly prized for construction and furniture-making, and has been used in traditional medicine to treat various ailments due to its medicinal properties. The wood of Shorea gardneri contains a compound called boswellic acid, which has anti-inflammatory properties, making it useful for relieving joint pain and reducing swelling. Herbal preparations of Shorea gardneri include extracts and oils, which can be used topically to soothe skin irritations and promote wound healing, or taken internally to support digestive health.
